steps.title: Identify the role of the underlined sentence
steps.content: The underlined sentence — "However, Allan and colleagues' study relied on a mean sample size of only about 18 fish" — is positioned between Allan's conclusion (previous sentence) and Sundin's review (following sentences). The word However signals a contrast or qualification, and the content (small sample size) is a methodological limitation. The question asks what function this sentence serves in the text as a whole.

steps.title: Eliminate each choice by checking what follows the underlined sentence
steps.content:
• Choice A — 'It introduces a detail about Allan and colleagues' study that is addressed in the sentences that follow.': The underlined sentence flags the small sample size (~18 fish), and the very next sentence (Sundin's review) directly addresses why small sample sizes are problematic. The detail is introduced and then explained. ✓
  • Choice B — 'It offers an alternative explanation for the behavior of Pomacentrus amboinensis that is described in the previous sentence.': The underlined sentence says nothing about an alternative explanation for the fish's behavior — it comments on the study's methodology, not on what causes the fish to behave a certain way. ✗
  • Choice C — 'It emphasizes the threat of ocean acidification to Pomacentrus amboinensis that is mentioned in the previous sentence.': A small sample size is a limitation of the study, not a statement about the environmental threat to the fish. The sentence weakens confidence in Allan's finding rather than emphasizing it. ✗
  • Choice D — 'It states the conclusion reached by Allan and colleagues that is elaborated on in the sentences that follow.': Allan's conclusion (strong effect of acidification on behavior) is stated in the previous sentence, not the underlined one. The underlined sentence identifies a methodological weakness, not a conclusion. ✗
That matches choice A — A is correct.
